title: Pleurospermum lindleyanum
taxonomy: Pleurospermum lindleyanum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apiaceae, commonly known as the carrot or parsley family. It is native to the Himalayan region of India and Nepal. The plant is also known as Lindley's carrot or Lindley's parsley fern due to its fern-like appearance. Within the Apiaceae family, Pleurospermum lindleyanum is placed in the genus Pleurospermum, which is a group of plants that are native to the Himalayas and surrounding regions. Further classification of the species includes its placement in the order Apiales.

Its roots and leaves are said to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help in reducing pain and inflammation. intro: Pleurospermum lindleyanum is a perennial plant species belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae), native to New Zealand and Australia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, which may aid in relieving pain, reducing inflammation, and promoting wound healing. Bioactive constituents of Pleurospermum lindleyanum include sesquiterpene lactones, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is used to treat various health conditions, including skin irritations, gastrointestinal issues, and respiratory problems.
